title: rapanea capitellata
intro: Rapanea capitellata, also known as the Māori pepper tree, is a species of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional Māori medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves and berries of the Rapanea capitellata plant have been used to treat various health issues, such as respiratory problems, skin conditions, and digestive issues, providing relief from symptoms like congestion, itching, and stomach pain. The plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and can also be used as a poultice or applied topically to affected areas.
